text.title: ¿En verdad COREA DEL SUR es tan increíble como pensamos? | Seúl 🇰🇷
text.u: Look at this, it's beautiful and how crazy as everybody look at the world on their phones.
A lot of people, many stores, trade of all kinds, colors, signs, I'm liking this.
[Music]
Friends, friends! (Speaks korean) that was Korean for "hello friends, welcome to South Korea"
That's right, that's right, this is an adventure I've been looking forward to do
for several years now- When I found out that South Korea was open again to receive tourists
I bought my ticket at that very moment and here we are in Seoul capital of South Korea
Known for being the technology capital of the world
South Korea as of the date I record this, has a population of just over 51 million people
Its official language is Korean , its currency is the Korean Won which the conversion is a little bit complicated
But it would be something like 10,000 won is 7.50 DLLS
A very famous country for its music, for its not so recent boom in the K-Pop
For its television series the K-Drama,
Its cinema has also for some time now has also made a lot of noise
South Korea is also known for its makeup
For their fashion
For their technology, for their their gastronomy
It goes without saying this is going to be a great epic adventure
We start this trip in Seoul the capital city, in the area of Dongdaemun which is this beauty that is at my backs
The correct pronunciation is... (Speaks korean) Did I say it right?
Today we are going to give an overview, let's let us be surprised by the first impressions we get from this beautiful territory
Normally I would say "let's hit it" but we're in Korea so we're saying (Speaks korean)
[Music]
So let's hit it, let's hit it. Let's get to know the subway, sounds good? Sounds like a good idea?
Something tells me that the subway here is sure to be really awesome.
I really like that the signs are lights
We are going to cover up the sign, oh no! Where's the sign?
Was erased? What happened? No... It's a reflector
Well, let's go, let's go, let's meet
This is very cool!
Most of the subway stations in fact are not just that
They're also some kind of small shopping malls
Actually, they're not that small
Threre's a few that look like real massive shopping malls, there are from three floors down
You can really found every brand you can think of, cheap or expensive, every kind
It's a really impressive trade happening here
Here we're seeing an example, we found a coffee shop, convenience store, storage areas for luggage, etc. etc.
But here, this goes on all the way there, and really you'll find everything
From a portable electric fan to Gucci shoes
You can find a lot of things
In the subways of seoul
By the way, since we're here, let's quickly see what's there in a convenience store
Obviously, lots and lots of energy drinks
See the variaty of energy drinks
It's real common on East Asia, they really like energy drinks
Here there are delicacies of all kinds
Some little coffee
Look, here we're also buying the subway cards
How do you say "thank you"? (Speaks korean)
Is difficult, Korean is difficult
See I picked the casanova bear one
OMG! Check how many instant soups
Yum! Like this video if you would like a video tasting only of instant korean soups
It will make a grat video and pleasing too
I can't wait
Oh here, there's an oreo fever, they have different flavors look
A blue one, triple chocolate, the classic ones, red velvet cake,
With more chocolate, a complete fever and that this one only a little shop
Look at these Cheetos, I already want to try this, uff!
I have to make a special video about convenience stores, right?
Yes, we agree, a convenience store video is coming soon
This is how the card is recharged at South Korea
By the way, meet Jeks, she's our guide. Say hi, my dear Jeks
Yeah! Our dear Jeks
An we're with Arisita, also
- Here's my subway card
This cards have animals, little animals
So check this, we put the card in here, right? It goes there
And you recharge it, how much you want? 10
I remembered how to say it
Then I stuck my hand
By the way, this how money looks like
This guy's name is Sejong, who is one of the creators of korean language, ok?
We put this in here
Here you go
There it is!
So here's recharging, gluck, gluck, gluck
One moment please, your card is recharging
And there you go
So now we'll go deep inside the city
Check this, we trake our card and put it on top
Ohhh...
And look at this, the doors on the subway get completly closed
Wow! This, of course, is to prevent many things, to prevent accidents, to prevent people from going on the tracks
Very unfortunate things that sadly do happen
We have here some of the little beverage machines just like the ones in Japan
See this reminds me a lot a lot a lot of Japan
And, it's a very curious fact that I love, I've told it many times in the channel
Most of the garbage cans that we go to see will be transparent this is because many years ago
In Japan there were a terrorist attack in which some gas bombs were placed in the garbage dumps and many people died
So that's why the garbage cans are transparent in many in many east asian countries
What do you think? Uh?
Maybe you already seen it in the channel, I have told it many times but it's very interesting and above all, it's a very disturbed data
The wagon has arrived, the wagon has arrived,
Let's go up!
Wow! Hey it's like a very wide wagon
How can I show it to you? How can I make an example?
Maybe with steps, let's check, from here to there is one, two, three, oh!
Four, five,
There's eight steps, about 2,50 meters, approx.
Around 2.50 meters wide, yeah, it's a very wide wagon, very clean, very pretty
And above all, it's really quiet
I even feel bad speaking this loud
It's a calm, relaxed, wagon and very very clean
It's important to mention that you won't always find it like this clean, everyone sittng
How do I open this? Like this?
I'm told that at rush hour, you get to find a lot of people standing around, it's not this quiet all the time cubans how many lines how
But, I think still is a great example of how this works
Hey! Check this out! How nice and how crazy that everybody is on their phones
Wich means there's goog signal down here on the subway
We even have open Wi-Fi down here for free use
You pay around 1,200 wons for every subway ticket
Wich is around .85 cents of a dollar
Around 19 mexican pesos
The subway actually covers most of South Korean capital surface
Check this
Look how many lines, how many stations
What do you think, uh? Take a look
Very pretty
Very tidy
Very nice
Seoul's subway, South Korea's capital
(MUSIC)
And see, in this subway station you can better appreciate what I was telling you, check this!
Ok, so the subway is over there, ok? We're coming from over there, we walk nothing, and see here, right here
The subway turns into a whole shopping mall, a whole big mall, take a look
Wow! Do you want a french bread? Some discount shoes or you want a dress or a blouse?
Do you want? Well, whatever you want, really this one it's huge, goes way beyond there
There and there. It's massive and it's not a one time thing, really
This happens in many stations, near from where we're staying
There's like two, right? Two that are very huge. It's really impressive and you get to see a lot of this
In Japan and in China then I guess it's something like East Asian countries
The developed countries in this part of the world
Wow! Just look at it! The beauty shops
It's very interesting, you know, like to be walking in an eternal japanese store, I feel like that
Every store looks pretty much the same for my very occidental eyes
Anothe beauty store over here
Some clothes, looks very pretty
Baby clothes
Wow!
It seems to go on and on and on and on and on and on
[Music]
Here's a couple of fun facts about South Korea
Fact number 1: People here are one year older
Because when they are born they are already born being 1 year old, so if you have a friend in South Korea
Who is exactly the same age as you are, if you are 17 he's going to be 18
If you're 34 he's going to be 35
This is so confusing, they're about to take it down
They're about to revoke
Maybe for next year it can change
Fun fact number 2: did you know that p*rn is illegal in South Korea
It's a true fact, completely true and real, see here we try to open a website
And it comes up an ad that says this is illegal, is forbidden because the site is improper
The principle for this is that it is believed that the young people should focus on their studies one hundred
And not be distracted by watching p*rn on a screen
I think it's very curious and a little bit hilarious
I would imagine that the young people here just use a vpn on their phone and just like that, problem solved! It's not complicated at all
Well, come over here, now we come to Gangnam
There it is, Gangnam style the little dance, of course
Remember you guys the Gangnam Style? This super macro trend, like a real world trend of about 10 years ago Opppa gangnam style
And the guy danced this way, it was so popular he has his own monument in the Gangnam neighborhood
In fact, the song is done for the district of Gangnam in Seoul
Which is like one of the most wealthy districts here, the most fancy.
And wow! Gangnam district is divine, a lot of people, a lot of stores, trade of all kinds, colorful signs
I am liking here! Look at this! Look at this streets, see these buildings
I think still my favorite area of the ones we've seen if I ever get too crazy and want to move to South Korea, Gangnam is where I want to live, we'll be here
Here I will be an outsider in South Korea
Something I have to mention, people in South Korea smoke a lot, really
In the convenience stores you see a lot of variety of cigarettes, they smoke a lot, but you can't smoke anywhere in the streets but only in designated areas
See, this is one of them, we are going to enter
So check this, here people come have a little smoke, the vaporizer, whatever and off you go
But only in these designated spaces
No, no, really I declare myself a fan of the Gangnam area.
Check this, it's full of bars and restaurants,
They say that here at night is where the fun's at, here are the bars here are the nightclubs here is the life
Yes yes this is my area, this is my environment I want to take pictures in every corner
Gangnam is my zone, ok? So far, Gangnam is gaining a space in my heart in Seoul
(Music)
Oh my god! Check this tremendous structure, this is Seoul's digital plaza
It's arquitechture is impressive, they always have very interesting exhibits, but above all I find it fascinating
Because it tells us an interesting part of the history of South Korea. You will see:
South Korea, although it doesn't look like it at all, is a relatively relatively a very new country. We are talking about
They gained their independence in 1945 when the armed forces of the
Japan surrendered in 1945. Wow! less than 100 years of being fully independent and the reason
This place tells us an important part of that history, as you will see, this used to be a stadium a very very symbolic baseball stadium
Since many, many, many years ago.
It used to have a Japanese name, and after the Japanese retired
It was renamed to the Seoul stadium, then to the Dongdaemun stadium, which is the area
And now it has became a public square, for that people come here to hang out and be amazed with the Digital Square displays
One of the architects in charge of the design of this Square was Zaha Hadid, a super famous Iraqi architect, who lost her life a few years ago
And is now one of the great icons of Seoul
Hey, folks who are watching this video, maybe you think you have seen big shopping malls, well I can assure you that they are not as big as those in South Korea
Check this out!
Wow! How many floors are those? Filled with just stores. Check this style and design
Not even Dubai dared to so much
I have gone to the supposedly world's largest mall in Dubai
I think this is more shocking, I don't know, I don't know
[Music]
Uff! So, we had a few drinks, we had a delicious dinner. Everything is really fascinating, even going to the bar is a very interesting experience
How they interact, wow! I'm fascinated by it all
I'm stunned, I really want to record everything, really
So stay tuned because there are a lot of super interesting videos coming of this journey, that I'm sure it's going to fascinate all of us equally
Let me know anyway if you have any suggestions of something I should see here in South Korea, I'm going to be a while
I'm going to be a little over a month here
So if you have any suggestions, video ideas, whatever, leave them below at comments and I will do my best to make them
Thanks for joining me, it was an excellent first day in South Korea, in the beautiful city of Seoul
I'm really tired, I have to go to rest to make the most of tomorrow
Of course we will see each other, as usual, in a few days with a new video. I proceed to try to say goodbye in Korean
[Music]
